text.Rotherham: As one worthy to be praised, called I on Yahweh,—And, from my foes, was I saved.
text.KJV: 22:4 I will call on the Lord, who is worthy to be praised: so shall I be saued from mine enemies.
text.NABRE: Praised be the Lord, I exclaim! I have been delivered from my enemies.

text.NRSV: For the waves of death encompassed me, the torrents of perdition assailed me;
text.Rotherham: When the breakers of death had encompassed me,—the torrents of perdition, made me afraid,—
text.KJV: 22:5 When the waues of death compassed me: the floods of vngodly men made me afraid.
text.NABRE: The breakers of death surged round about me, the menacing floods terrified me;

text.NRSV: the cords of Sheol entangled me, the snares of death confronted me.
text.Rotherham: the meshes of hades, had surrounded me,—the snares of death had confronted me,
text.KJV: 22:6 The sorowes of Hell compassed me about: the snares of death preuented me.
text.NABRE: The cords of Sheol tightened; the snares of death lay in wait for me.

text.NRSV: In my distress I called upon the Lord; to my God I called. From his temple he heard my voice, and my cry came to his ears.
text.Rotherham: In my distress, called I on Yahweh, Yea, unto my God, did I call,—and he hearkened, out of his temple, unto my voice, and, my cry for help, was in his ears!
text.KJV: 22:7 In my distresse I called vpon the Lord, and cryed to my God, and hee did heare my voice out of his Temple, and my crie did enter into his eares.
text.NABRE: In my distress I called out: Lord! I cried out to my God; From his temple he heard my voice, my cry reached his ears.

text.NRSV: Then the earth reeled and rocked; the foundations of the heavens trembled and quaked, because he was angry.
text.Rotherham: Then did the earth shake and quake, the foundations of the heavens, were deeply moved,—yea they did shake, because he was angry,
text.KJV: 22:8 Then the earth shooke and trembled: the foundations of heauen mooued and shooke, because hee was wroth.
text.NABRE: The earth rocked and shook; the foundations of the heavens trembled; they shook as his wrath flared up.

text.NRSV: Smoke went up from his nostrils, and devouring fire from his mouth; glowing coals flamed forth from him.
text.Rotherham: There went up a smoke in his nostrils, and, a fire out of his mouth, devoured,—live coals, were kindled from it:
text.KJV: 22:9 There went vp a smoake out of his nostrils, and fire out of his mouth deuoured: coales were kindled by it.
text.NABRE: Smoke rose in his nostrils, a devouring fire from his mouth; it kindled coals into flame.

text.NRSV: He bowed the heavens, and came down; thick darkness was under his feet.
text.Rotherham: Then he stretched out the heavens, and came down,—and, thick gloom, was under his feet;
text.KJV: 22:10 Hee bowed the heauens also and came downe: and darkenesse was vnder his feete.
text.NABRE: He parted the heavens and came down, a dark cloud under his feet.

text.NRSV: He rode on a cherub, and flew; he was seen upon the wings of the wind.
text.Rotherham: Then he rode on a cherub, and flew,—and was seen on the wings of the wind;
text.KJV: 22:11 And he rode vpon a Cherub, and did flie: and hee was seene vpon the wings of the winde.
text.NABRE: Mounted on a cherub he flew, borne along on the wings of the wind.

text.NRSV: He made darkness around him a canopy, thick clouds, a gathering of water.
text.Rotherham: And made of the darkness around him, pavilions,—gathering of waters, clouds of vapours.
text.KJV: 22:12 And hee made darkenesse pauilions round about him, darke waters, and thicke clouds of the skies.
text.NABRE: He made darkness the cover about him, a mass of water, heavy thunderheads.

text.NRSV: Out of the brightness before him coals of fire flamed forth.
text.Rotherham: Out of the brightness before him, were kindled live coals of fire;
text.KJV: 22:13 Through the brightnesse before him, were coales of fire kindled.
text.NABRE: From the brightness of his presence coals were kindled to flame.

text.NRSV: The Lord thundered from heaven; the Most High uttered his voice.
text.Rotherham: Thunder from the heavens, did Yahweh give forth,—yea, the Highest, uttered his voice;
text.KJV: 22:14 The Lord thundred from heauen: and the most high vttered his voice.
text.NABRE: The Lord thundered from heaven; the Most High made his voice resound.

text.NRSV: He sent out arrows, and scattered them—lightning, and routed them.
text.Rotherham: And he sent forth arrows, and scattered them,—lightning, and confused them;
text.KJV: 22:15 And he sent out arrowes, and scattered them; lightning, and discomfited them.
text.NABRE: He let fly arrows and scattered them; lightning, and dispersed them.

text.NRSV: Then the channels of the sea were seen, the foundations of the world were laid bare at the rebuke of the Lord, at the blast of the breath of his nostrils.
text.Rotherham: Then appeared the channels of the sea, were uncovered the foundations of the world,—at the rebuke of Yahweh, the blast of the breath of his nostrils;
text.KJV: 22:16 And the channels of the Sea appeared, the foundations of the world were discouered, at the rebuking of the Lord, at the blast of the breath of his nostrils.
text.NABRE: Then the bed of the sea appeared; the world’s foundations lay bare, At the roar of the Lord, at the storming breath of his nostrils.

text.NRSV: He reached from on high, he took me, he drew me out of mighty waters.
text.Rotherham: He sent from on high, he took me,—he drew me out of many waters;
text.KJV: 22:17 He sent from aboue, he tooke me: he drew me out of many waters.
text.NABRE: He reached down from on high and seized me, drew me out of the deep waters.

text.NRSV: He delivered me from my strong enemy, from those who hated me; for they were too mighty for me.
text.Rotherham: He rescued me from my foe, in his might,—from them who hated me, because they were too strong for me:
text.KJV: 22:18 He deliuered me from my strong enemy, and from them that hated mee: for they were too strong for me.
text.NABRE: He rescued me from my mighty enemy, from foes too powerful for me.

text.NRSV: They came upon me in the day of my calamity, but the Lord was my stay.
text.Rotherham: They confronted me, in the day of my necessity,—then became Yahweh my stay:
text.KJV: 22:19 They preuented me in the day of my calamitie: but the Lord was my stay.
text.NABRE: They attacked me on a day of distress, but the Lord came to my support.

text.NRSV: He brought me out into a broad place; he delivered me, because he delighted in me.
text.Rotherham: And brought out, into a large place, even me,—he delivered me, because he delighted in me:
text.KJV: 22:20 Hee brought me forth also into a large place: he deliuered me, because hee delighted in me.
text.NABRE: He set me free in the open; he rescued me because he loves me.

text.NRSV: The Lord rewarded me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands he recompensed me.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh rewarded me, according to my righteousness—according to the cleanness of my hands, he repaid me:
text.KJV: 22:21 The Lord rewarded mee according to my righteousnesse: according to the cleannesse of my hands, hath hee recompensed me.
text.NABRE: The Lord acknowledged my righteousness; rewarded my clean hands.

text.NRSV: For I have kept the ways of the Lord, and have not wickedly departed from my God.
text.Rotherham: For I had kept the ways of Yahweh,—and not broken away from my God;
text.KJV: 22:22 For I haue kept the wayes of the Lord, and haue not wickedly departed from my God.
text.NABRE: For I kept the ways of the Lord; I was not disloyal to my God.

text.NRSV: For all his ordinances were before me, and from his statutes I did not turn aside.
text.Rotherham: For, all his regulations, were before me,—and, as for his statutes, I turned not from them.
text.KJV: 22:23 For all his iudgements were before me: and as for his Statutes, I did not depart from them.
text.NABRE: His laws were all before me, his decrees I did not cast aside.

text.NRSV: I was blameless before him, and I kept myself from guilt.
text.Rotherham: So became I blameless towards him,—and kept myself from mine iniquity:
text.KJV: 22:24 I was also vpright before him: and haue kept my selfe from mine iniquitie.
text.NABRE: I was honest toward him; I was on guard against sin.

text.NRSV: Therefore the Lord has recompensed me according to my righteousness, according to my cleanness in his sight.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh therefore repaid me, according to my righteousness,—according to my pureness before his eyes.
text.KJV: 22:25 Therefore the Lord hath recompensed me, according to my righteousnesse: according to my cleannesse in his eye sight.
text.NABRE: So the Lord rewarded my righteousness, the cleanness of my hands in his sight.

text.NRSV: With the loyal you show yourself loyal; with the blameless you show yourself blameless;
text.Rotherham: With the loving, thou didst show thyself loving,—with the blameless hero, thou didst show thyself blameless;
text.KJV: 22:26 With the merciful thou wilt shew thy selfe mercifull, and with the vpright man thou wilt shew thy selfe vpright.
text.NABRE: Toward the faithful you are faithful; to the honest you are honest;

text.NRSV: with the pure you show yourself pure, and with the crooked you show yourself perverse.
text.Rotherham: With the pure, thou didst show thyself pure,—but, with the perverse, thou didst shew thyself ready to contend:
text.KJV: 22:27 With the pure thou wilt shew thy selfe pure: and with the froward, thou wilt shew thy selfe vnsauoury.
text.NABRE: Toward the sincere you are sincere; but to the perverse you are devious.

text.NRSV: You deliver a humble people, but your eyes are upon the haughty to bring them down.
text.Rotherham: And, a patient people, thou didst save,—but, thine eyes, were on the lofty—thou layedst them low;
text.KJV: 22:28 And the afflicted people thou wilt saue: but thine eyes are vpon the hautie, that thou mayest bring them downe.
text.NABRE: Humble people you save, though on the haughty your eyes look down.

text.NRSV: Indeed, you are my lamp, O Lord, the Lord lightens my darkness.
text.Rotherham: For, thou, wast my lamp, O Yahweh,—and, Yahweh, enlightened my darkness;
text.KJV: 22:29 For thou art my lampe, O Lord: and the Lord wil lighten my darkenesse.
text.NABRE: You are my lamp, O Lord! My God brightens the darkness about me.

text.NRSV: By you I can crush a troop, and by my God I can leap over a wall.
text.Rotherham: For, by thee, I ran through a troop,—by my God, I leapt over a wall.
text.KJV: 22:30 For by thee I haue run through a troupe: by my God haue I leaped ouer a wall.
text.NABRE: With you I can rush an armed band, with my God to help I can leap a wall.

text.NRSV: This God—his way is perfect; the promise of the Lord proves true; he is a shield for all who take refuge in him.
text.Rotherham: As for God, blameless is his way,—the speech of Yahweh, hath been proved, a shield, he is to all who seek refuge in him.
text.KJV: 22:31 As for God, his way is perfect, the word of the Lord is tried: he is a buckler to all them that trust in him.
text.NABRE: God’s way is unerring; the Lord ’s promise is tried and true; he is a shield for all who trust in him.

text.NRSV: For who is God, but the Lord? And who is a rock, except our God?
text.Rotherham: For who is a GOD, save Yahweh? and who a Rock, save our God?
text.KJV: 22:32 For who is God, saue the Lord ? and who is a rocke, saue our God?
text.NABRE: Truly, who is God except the Lord? Who but our God is the rock?

text.NRSV: The God who has girded me with strength has opened wide my path.
text.Rotherham: GOD, is my fortress of strength,—and shewed to the blameless his way;
text.KJV: 22:33 God is my strength and power: and he maketh my way perfect.
text.NABRE: This God who girded me with might, kept my way unerring,

text.NRSV: He made my feet like the feet of deer, and set me secure on the heights.
text.Rotherham: Planting my feet like the hinds’,—yea, on my high places, he caused me to stand;
text.KJV: 22:34 Hee maketh my feet like hindes feet: and setteth mee vpon my high places.
text.NABRE: Who made my feet swift as a deer’s, set me safe on the heights,

text.NRSV: He trains my hands for war, so that my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
text.Rotherham: Teaching my hands, to war,—so that a bow of bronze was bent by mine arms;
text.KJV: 22:35 He teacheth my hands to warre: so that a bow of steele is broken by mine armes.
text.NABRE: Who trained my hands for war, my arms to bend even a bow of bronze.

text.NRSV: You have given me the shield of your salvation, and your help has made me great.
text.Rotherham: Thus didst thou grant me, as a shield, thy salvation,—and, thy condescension, made me great.
text.KJV: 22:36 Thou hast also giuen mee the shield of thy saluation: and thy gentlenesse hath made me great.
text.NABRE: You have given me your protecting shield, and your help has made me great.

text.NRSV: You enlarged my steps under me, and my feet did not slip.
text.Rotherham: Thou didst widen my stepping-places, under me,—so that mine ankles faltered not:
text.KJV: 22:37 Thou hast enlarged my steps vnder me: so that my feet did not slip.
text.NABRE: You gave me room to stride; my feet never stumbled.

text.NRSV: I pursued my enemies and destroyed them, and did not turn back until they were consumed.
text.Rotherham: I pursued my foes, and destroyed them,—and returned not, till they were consumed;
text.KJV: 22:38 I haue pursued mine enemies, and destroyed them: and turned not againe vntill I had consumed them.
text.NABRE: I pursued my enemies and overtook them; I did not turn back till I destroyed them.

text.NRSV: I consumed them; I struck them down, so that they did not rise; they fell under my feet.
text.Rotherham: So I consumed them, and crushed them, and they rose not again,—thus fell they under my feet:
text.KJV: 22:39 And I haue consumed them and wounded them, that they could not arise: yea, they are fallen vnder my feet.
text.NABRE: I struck them down, and they did not rise; they fell dead at my feet.

text.NRSV: For you girded me with strength for the battle; you made my assailants sink under me.
text.Rotherham: Thus didst thou gird me with strength, for the battle,—thou subduedst mine assailants under me:
text.KJV: 22:40 For thou hast girded mee with strength to battel: them that rose vp against me, hast thou subdued vnder me.
text.NABRE: You girded me with strength for war; subdued adversaries at my feet.

text.NRSV: You made my enemies turn their backs to me, those who hated me, and I destroyed them.
text.Rotherham: And, as for my foes, thou didst give me their neck,—yea, them who hated me, that I might destroy them:
text.KJV: 22:41 Thou hast also giuen mee the necks of mine enemies, that I might destroy them that hate me.
text.NABRE: My foes you put to flight before me; those who hated me I destroyed.

text.NRSV: They looked, but there was no one to save them; they cried to the Lord, but he did not answer them.
text.Rotherham: They cried out, but there was none to save,—unto Yahweh, but he answered them not.
text.KJV: 22:42 They looked, but there was none to saue: euen vnto the Lord, but he answered them not.
text.NABRE: They cried for help, but no one saved them, cried to the Lord but got no answer.

text.NRSV: I beat them fine like the dust of the earth, I crushed them and stamped them down like the mire of the streets.
text.Rotherham: Then did I beat them in pieces, like the dust of the earth,—like the clay of the lanes, did I crush them, stamp them down.
text.KJV: 22:43 Then did I beat them as small as the dust of the earth: I did stampe them as the myre of the street, and did spread them abroad.
text.NABRE: I ground them fine as the dust of the earth; like mud in the streets I trod them down.

text.NRSV: You delivered me from strife with the peoples; you kept me as the head of the nations; people whom I had not known served me.
text.Rotherham: Thus didst thou rescue me from the contentions of my people, didst keep me to be the head of nations:—a people whom I had not known, served me;
text.KJV: 22:44 Thou also hast deliuered mee from the striuings of my people, thou hast kept mee to be head of the heathen: a people which I knew not, shall serue me.
text.NABRE: You rescued me from the strife of my people; you made me head over nations. A people I had not known became my slaves;

text.NRSV: Foreigners came cringing to me; as soon as they heard of me, they obeyed me.
text.Rotherham: The sons of the foreigner, came cringing unto me,—at the hearing of the ear, they submitted to me,—
text.KJV: 22:45 Strangers shall submit themselues vnto me: as soone as they heare, they shall be obedient vnto me.
text.NABRE: Foreigners cringed before me; as soon as they heard of me they obeyed.

text.NRSV: Foreigners lost heart, and came trembling out of their strongholds.
text.Rotherham: The sons of the foreigner, were disheartened, and came quaking, out of their fortresses.
text.KJV: 22:46 Strangers shall fade away: and they shall bee afraid out of their close places.
text.NABRE: Their courage failed; they came trembling from their fortresses.

text.NRSV: The Lord lives! Blessed be my rock, and exalted be my God, the rock of my salvation,
text.Rotherham: Yahweh liveth, and blessed be my rock,—and exalted be the God (of the rock) of my salvation:—
text.KJV: 22:47 The Lord liueth, and blessed be my rocke: and exalted be the God of the rocke of my saluation.
text.NABRE: The Lord lives! Blessed be my rock! Exalted be God, the rock of my salvation.

text.NRSV: the God who gave me vengeance and brought down peoples under me,
text.Rotherham: The GOD who hath avenged me, and brought down peoples under me;
text.KJV: 22:48 It is God that auengeth mee, and that bringeth downe the people vnder me:
text.NABRE: O God who granted me vindication, subdued peoples under me,

text.NRSV: who brought me out from my enemies; you exalted me above my adversaries, you delivered me from the violent.
text.Rotherham: And brought me forth from among my foes,—yea, from mine assailants, hast thou set me on high, from the man of violence, hast thou delivered me.
text.KJV: 22:49 And that bringeth me forth from mine enemies: thou also hast lifted mee vp on high aboue them that rose vp against me: thou hast deliuered me from the violent man.
text.NABRE: and helped me escape from my enemies, Truly you have exalted me above my adversaries, from the violent you have rescued me.

text.NRSV: For this I will extol you, O Lord, among the nations, and sing praises to your name.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, will I praise thee, O Yahweh, among the nations,—and, to thy name, will I touch the strings:—
text.KJV: 22:50 Therefore I will giue thankes vnto thee, O Lord, among the heathen: and I will sing praises vnto thy Name.
text.NABRE: Thus I will proclaim you, Lord, among the nations; I will sing the praises of your name.

text.NRSV: Great triumphs he gives to his king, and shows steadfast love to his anointed, to David and his descendants forever.
text.Rotherham: Who hath made great the victories of his King,—and shown lovingkindness to his Anointed One, to David and to his Seed, unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: 22:51 He is the towre of saluation for his king: and sheweth mercy to his Anointed, vnto Dauid, and to his seede for euermore.
text.NABRE: You have given great victories to your king, and shown kindness to your anointed, to David and his posterity forever.

text.NRSV: Now these are the last words of David: The oracle of David, son of Jesse, the oracle of the man whom God exalted, the anointed of the God of Jacob, the favorite of the Strong One of Israel:
text.Rotherham: Now, these, are the last words of David,—The oracle of David, son of Jesse, Yea the oracle of The man raised up on high, The Anointed of the God of Jacob, the Delight of the Songs of Israel:
text.KJV: 23:1 Nowe these bee the last words of Dauid: Dauid the sonne of Iesse saide, and the man who was raised vp on high, the Anointed of the God of Iacob, and the sweet Psalmist of Israel, said,
text.NABRE: Chapter 23 The Last Words of David. These are the last words of David: The oracle of David, son of Jesse; the oracle of the man God raised up, Anointed of the God of Jacob, favorite of the Mighty One of Israel.

text.NRSV: The spirit of the Lord speaks through me, his word is upon my tongue.
text.Rotherham: The Spirit of Yahweh, spake in me,—And, his word, was on my tongue;
text.KJV: 23:2 The spirit of the Lord spake by me, and his word was in my tongue.
text.NABRE: The spirit of the Lord spoke through me; his word was on my tongue.

text.NRSV: The God of Israel has spoken, the Rock of Israel has said to me: One who rules over people justly, ruling in the fear of God,
text.Rotherham: Said the God of Israel, Unto me, spake the Rock of Israel:—One Ruling over Men, A Righteous One, ruling in the reverence of God,
text.KJV: 23:3 The God of Israel said, the Rocke of Israel spake to me: he that ruleth ouer men must be iust, ruling in the feare of God:
text.NABRE: The God of Israel spoke; of me the Rock of Israel said, “One who rules over humankind with justice, who rules in the fear of God,

text.NRSV: is like the light of morning, like the sun rising on a cloudless morning, gleaming from the rain on the grassy land.
text.Rotherham: Is even as the light of the morning when ariseth the sun,—A morning, without clouds, [As] from brightness, [and] from rain, the fresh shoots out of the earth.
text.KJV: 23:4 And he shall be as the light of the morning, when the Sunne riseth, euen a morning, without cloudes; as the tender grasse springing out of the earth by cleare shining after raine:
text.NABRE: Is like the light at sunrise on a cloudless morning, making the land’s vegetation glisten after rain.”

text.NRSV: Is not my house like this with God? For he has made with me an everlasting covenant, ordered in all things and secure. Will he not cause to prosper all my help and my desire?
text.Rotherham: When, not so, was my house with GOD, Then, a covenant age-abiding, he appointed me, Ordered in all things and guarded, Now that it is all my salvation and all my desire, Will he not make it shoot forth?
text.KJV: 23:5 Although my house be not so with God: yet he hath made with mee an euerlasting couenant, ordred in al things and sure: for this is all my saluation, and all my desire, although he make it not to grow.
text.NABRE: Is not my house firm before God? He has made an eternal covenant with me, set forth in detail and secured. Will he not bring to fruition all my salvation and my every desire?

text.NRSV: But the godless are all like thorns that are thrown away; for they cannot be picked up with the hand;
text.Rotherham: But, as for the abandoned, like thorns to be tossed away are they all,—For, not with the hand, can they be taken;
text.KJV: 23:6 ¶ But the sonnes of Belial shall bee all of them as thornes thrust away, because they cannot be taken with hands,
text.NABRE: But the wicked are all like thorns to be cast away; they cannot be taken up by hand.

text.NRSV: to touch them one uses an iron bar or the shaft of a spear. And they are entirely consumed in fire on the spot.
text.Rotherham: But, the man that would touch them, Must fence himself with iron, and the shaft of a spear,—Then, with fire, shall they be, consumed, on the spot!
text.KJV: 23:7 But the man that shal touch them, must be fenced with yron, and the staffe of a speare, and they shall bee vtterly burnt with fire in the same place.
text.NABRE: One wishing to touch them must be armed with iron or the shaft of a spear. They must be utterly consumed by fire.

text.NRSV: These are the names of the warriors whom David had: Josheb-basshebeth a Tahchemonite; he was chief of the Three; he wielded his spear against eight hundred whom he killed at one time.
text.Rotherham: These, are the names of the mighty men, who belonged to David,—The president a Tachmonite head of the charioteers, the same, was Adino the Eznite, for eight hundred, slain at one time.
text.KJV: 23:8 ¶ These be the names of the mightie men whome Dauid had: The Tachmonite that sate in the seat, chiefe among the captaines, (the same was Adino the Eznite:) hee lift vp his speare against eight hundred, whom he slew at one time.
text.NABRE: David’s Warriors. These are the names of David’s warriors. Ishbaal, the son of Hachamoni, chief of the Three. He brandished his spear over eight hundred whom he had slain in a single encounter.

text.NRSV: Next to him among the three warriors was Eleazar son of Dodo son of Ahohi. He was with David when they defied the Philistines who were gathered there for battle. The Israelites withdrew,
text.Rotherham: And, after him, Eleazar son of Dodo, son of Ahohi,—in the hero-class of mighty men, with David, when they reproached the Philistines, they were gathered together there to battle, but the men of Israel had gone up;
text.KJV: 23:9 And after him was Eleazar the sonne of Dodo the Ahohite, one of the three mightie men with Dauid, when they defied the Philistines that were there gathered together to battell, and the men of Israel were gone away.
text.NABRE: Next to him was Eleazar, the son of Dodo the Ahohite, one of the Three warriors with David at Ephes-dammim, when they insulted the Philistines who had massed there for battle. The Israelites had retreated,

text.NRSV: but he stood his ground. He struck down the Philistines until his arm grew weary, though his hand clung to the sword. The Lord brought about a great victory that day. Then the people came back to him—but only to strip the dead.
text.Rotherham: he, however, arose and smote among the Philistines until his hand was weary, and his hand clave unto his sword, so Yahweh wrought a great victory on that day,—the people, coming back after him, only to strip the slain.
text.KJV: 23:10 He arose, and smote the Philistines vntill his hand was wearie, and his hand claue vnto the sword: and the Lord wrought a great victorie that day, and the people returned after him onely to spoile.
text.NABRE: but he stood there and struck down the Philistines until his hand grew tired from clutching the sword. The Lord brought about a great victory on that day; the army turned back to rejoin Eleazar, but only to strip the slain.

text.NRSV: Next to him was Shammah son of Agee, the Hararite. The Philistines gathered together at Lehi, where there was a plot of ground full of lentils; and the army fled from the Philistines.
text.Rotherham: And, after him, Shammah son of Agee, the Hararite,—and, when the Philistines were gathered together into a troop, there being at hand an allotment of field-land full of lentils, but, the people, having fled from the face of the Philistines,
text.KJV: 23:11 And after him was Shammah the sonne of Agee the Hararite: and the Philistines were gathered together into a troupe, where was a piece of ground full of lentiles: and the people fled from the Philistines.
text.NABRE: Next to him was Shammah, son of Agee the Hararite. The Philistines had assembled at Lehi, where there was a plot of land full of lentils. The people were fleeing before the Philistines,

text.NRSV: But he took his stand in the middle of the plot, defended it, and killed the Philistines; and the Lord brought about a great victory.
text.Rotherham: then took he his stand in the midst of the allotment, and defended it, and smote the Philistines,—and Yahweh wrought a great victory.
text.KJV: 23:12 But hee stood in the midst of the ground, and defended it, and slewe the Philistines: and the Lord wrought a great victorie.
text.NABRE: but he took his stand in the middle of the plot, kept it safe, and cut down the Philistines. Thus the Lord brought about a great victory. Such deeds as these the Three warriors performed.

text.NRSV: Towards the beginning of harvest three of the thirty chiefs went down to join David at the cave of Adullam, while a band of Philistines was encamped in the valley of Rephaim.
text.Rotherham: And three of the thirty chiefs descended, and came in, towards harvest, unto David, unto the cave of Adullam,—although, a troop of Philistines, were encamped in the vale of Rephaim;
text.KJV: 23:13 And three of the thirtie chiefe went downe and came to Dauid in the haruest time, vnto the caue of Adullam: and the troupe of the Philistines pitched in the valley of Rephaim.
text.NABRE: Three of the Thirty chiefs went down to David in the cave of Adullam during the harvest, while a Philistine clan was encamped in the Valley of Rephaim.

text.NRSV: David was then in the stronghold; and the garrison of the Philistines was then at Bethlehem.
text.Rotherham: and, David, then was in a stronghold,—and, a garrison of Philistines, was then in Bethlehem.
text.KJV: 23:14 And Dauid was then in an holde, and the garison of the Philistines was then in Bethlehem.
text.NABRE: David was then in the stronghold, and there was a garrison of Philistines in Bethlehem.

text.NRSV: David said longingly, “O that someone would give me water to drink from the well that is by the gate of Bethlehem!”
text.Rotherham: And David longed, and said,—Who will give me to drink water out of the well of Bethlehem, that is within the gate?
text.KJV: 23:15 And Dauid longed, and said, Oh that one would giue mee drinke of the water of the well of Bethlehem which is by the gate.
text.NABRE: Now David had a craving and said, “If only someone would give me a drink of water from the cistern by the gate of Bethlehem!”

text.NRSV: Then the three warriors broke through the camp of the Philistines, drew water from the well that is by the gate of Bethlehem, and brought it to David. But he would not drink of it; he poured it out to the Lord,
text.Rotherham: And the three mighty men brake through the camp of Philistines, and drew water out of the well of Bethlehem, that was within the gate, and bare it, and brought it in unto David,—yet would he not drink, but poured it out unto Yahweh,
text.KJV: 23:16 And the three mightie men brake through the host of the Philistines, and drew water out of the Well of Bethlehem, that was by the gate, and tooke it, and brought it to Dauid: neuerthelesse he would not drinke thereof, but powred it out vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: Thereupon the three warriors broke through the encampment of the Philistines, drew water from the cistern by the gate of Bethlehem, and carried it back to David. But he refused to drink it, and instead poured it out to the Lord,

text.NRSV: for he said, “The Lord forbid that I should do this. Can I drink the blood of the men who went at the risk of their lives?” Therefore he would not drink it. The three warriors did these things.
text.Rotherham: and said—Be it far from me, O Yahweh, that I should do this! is it not the blood of the men who went with their lives [in their hands] ? So he would not drink it. These things, did the three mighty men.
text.KJV: 23:17 And he said, Be it farre from me, O Lord, that I should doe this: is not this the blood of the men that went in ieopardie of their liues? therefore he would not drinke it. These things did these three mightie men.
text.NABRE: saying: “The Lord forbid that I do such a thing! Could I drink the blood of these men who went at the risk of their lives?” So he refused to drink it.

text.NRSV: Now Abishai son of Zeruiah, the brother of Joab, was chief of the Thirty. With his spear he fought against three hundred men and killed them, and won a name beside the Three.
text.Rotherham: And. Abishai brother of Joab son of Zeruiah, he, was chief of three, in that he brandished his spear against three hundred whom he slew; and, he, had a name among three.
text.KJV: 23:18 And Abishai the brother of Ioab, the sonne of Zeruiah, was chiefe among three, and he lift vp his speare against three hundred, and slew them, and had the name among three.
text.NABRE: Abishai, the brother of Joab, son of Zeruiah, was the chief of the Thirty; he brandished his spear over three hundred whom he had slain. He made a name among the Thirty,

text.NRSV: He was the most renowned of the Thirty, and became their commander; but he did not attain to the Three.
text.Rotherham: Was he not most honourable, of the three, and so became their captain? Nevertheless, unto the three, he attained not.
text.KJV: 23:19 Was hee not most honourable of three? therefore he was their captaine: howbeit, hee attained not vnto the first three.
text.NABRE: but was more famous than any of the Thirty, becoming their leader. However, he did not attain to the Three.

text.NRSV: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was a valiant warrior from Kabzeel, a doer of great deeds; he struck down two sons of Ariel of Moab. He also went down and killed a lion in a pit on a day when snow had fallen.
text.Rotherham: Benaiah also, son of Jehoiadah, son of an active man, hero of many a deed, a man of Kabzeel, he, smote the two sons of Ariel of Moab, he, also went down and smote a lion in the midst of a pit, on a day of snow;
text.KJV: 23:20 And Benaiah the sonne of Iehoiada the sonne of a valiant man, of Kabzeel, who had done many actes, he slew two lion-like men of Moab: hee went downe also, and slewe a Lyon in the middest of a pit in time of snow.
text.NABRE: Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, a valiant man of mighty deeds, from Kabzeel, killed the two sons of Ariel of Moab. Also, he went down and killed the lion in the cistern on a snowy day.

text.NRSV: And he killed an Egyptian, a handsome man. The Egyptian had a spear in his hand; but Benaiah went against him with a staff, snatched the spear out of the Egyptian’s hand, and killed him with his own spear.
text.Rotherham: he, also smote an Egyptian, who was of valiant bearing, and, in the hand of the Egyptian, was a spear, but he went down unto him with a staff,—and wrested the spear out of the hand of the Egyptian, and slew him with his own spear.
text.KJV: 23:21 And he slew an Egyptian a goodly man: and the Egyptian had a speare in his hand; but he went downe to him with a staffe, and plucked the speare out of the Egyptians hand, and slewe him with his owne speare.
text.NABRE: He likewise slew an Egyptian, a huge man. The Egyptian carried a spear, but Benaiah came against him with a staff; he wrested the spear from the Egyptian’s hand, and killed him with that spear.

text.NRSV: Such were the things Benaiah son of Jehoiada did, and won a name beside the three warriors.
text.Rotherham: These things, did Benaiah son of Jehoiada, and, he, had a name among three mighty men.
text.KJV: 23:22 These things did Benaiah the sonne of Iehoiada, and had the name among three mightie men.
text.NABRE: Such deeds as these Benaiah, the son of Jehoiada, performed; and he made a name among the Thirty warriors

text.NRSV: He was renowned among the Thirty, but he did not attain to the Three. And David put him in charge of his bodyguard.
text.Rotherham: He was the most honourable, of thirty, although, unto the three, he attained no,—so David added him to his council.
text.KJV: 23:23 Hee was more honourable then the thirtie, but hee attained not to the first three: and Dauid set him ouer his guard.
text.NABRE: but was more famous than any of the Thirty. However, he did not attain to the Three. David put him in charge of his bodyguard.

text.NRSV: Among the Thirty were Asahel brother of Joab; Elhanan son of Dodo of Bethlehem;
text.Rotherham: Asahel, brother of Joab, was among the thirty,—Elhanan, son of Dodo, of Bethlehem;
text.KJV: 23:24 Asahel the brother of Ioab was one of the thirtie: Elhanan the sonne of Dodo of Bethlehem,
text.NABRE: Asahel, brother of Joab, was among the Thirty; Elhanan, son of Dodo, from Bethlehem;

text.NRSV: Shammah of Harod; Elika of Harod;
text.Rotherham: Shammah, the Harodite; Elika, the Harodite;
text.KJV: 23:25 Shammah the Harodite, Elika the Harodite,
text.NABRE: Shammah, from En-harod; Elika, from En-harod;

text.NRSV: Helez the Paltite; Ira son of Ikkesh of Tekoa;
text.Rotherham: Helez, the Paltite, Ira, son of Ikkesh, the Tekoite;
text.KJV: 23:26 Helez the Paltite, Ira the sonne of Ikkesh the Tekoite,
text.NABRE: Helez, from Beth-pelet; Ira, son of Ikkesh, from Tekoa;

text.NRSV: Abiezer of Anathoth; Mebunnai the Hushathite;
text.Rotherham: Abiezer, the Anathothite, Mebunnai, the Hushathite;
text.KJV: 23:27 Abiezer the Anethothite, Mebunnai the Hushathite,
text.NABRE: Abiezer, from Anathoth; Sibbecai, from Husha;

text.NRSV: Zalmon the Ahohite; Maharai of Netophah;
text.Rotherham: Zalmon, the Ahohite, Maharai, the Netophathite;
text.KJV: 23:28 Zalmon the Ahohite, Maharai the Netophathite,
text.NABRE: Zalmon, from Ahoh; Maharai, from Netophah;

text.NRSV: Heleb son of Baanah of Netophah; Ittai son of Ribai of Gibeah of the Benjaminites;
text.Rotherham: Heleb, son of Baanah, the Netophathite,—Ittai, son of Ribai, of Gibeah, of the sons of Benjamin;
text.KJV: 23:29 Heleb the sonne of Baanah, a Netophathite, Ittai the sonne of Ribai out of Gibeah of the children of Beniamin.
text.NABRE: Heled, son of Baanah, from Netophah; Ittai, son of Ribai, from Gibeah of Benjamin;

text.NRSV: Benaiah of Pirathon; Hiddai of Gaash;
text.Rotherham: Benaiah, a Pirathonite, Hiddai, of the torrents of Gaash;
text.KJV: 23:30 Benaiah the Pirathonite, Hiddai of the brookes of Gaash,
text.NABRE: Benaiah, from Pirathon; Hiddai, from the valley of Gaash;

text.NRSV: Abi-albon the Arbathite; Azmaveth of Bahurim;
text.Rotherham: Abi-albon, the Arbathite, Azmaveth, the Barhumite;
text.KJV: 23:31 Abialbon the Arbathite, Azmaueth the Barhumite,
text.NABRE: Abibaal, from Beth-arabah; Azmaveth, from Bahurim;

text.NRSV: Eliahba of Shaalbon; the sons of Jashen: Jonathan,
text.Rotherham: Eliahba, the Shaalbonite, (Of) the sons of Jashen, Jonathan;
text.KJV: 23:32 Elihaba the Shaalbonite: of the sonnes of Iashen, Ionathan,
text.NABRE: Eliahba, from Shaalbon; Jashen the Gunite; Jonathan,

text.NRSV: son of Shammah the Hararite; Ahiam son of Sharar the Hararite;
text.Rotherham: Shammah, the Hararite, Ahiam, son of Sharar, the Ararite;
text.KJV: 23:33 Shammah the Hararite, Ahiam the sonne of Sharar the Hararite,
text.NABRE: son of Shammah the Hararite; Ahiam, son of Sharar the Hararite;

text.NRSV: Eliphelet son of Ahasbai of Maacah; Eliam son of Ahithophel the Gilonite;
text.Rotherham: Eliphelet, son of Ahasbai, son of the Maacathite. Eliam, son of Ahithophel, the Gilonite.
text.KJV: 23:34 Eliphelet the sonne of Ahasbai, the sonne of the Maachathite, Eliam the sonne of Ahithophel the Gilonite,
text.NABRE: Eliphelet, son of Ahasbai, from Beth-maacah; Eliam, son of Ahithophel, from Gilo;

text.NRSV: Hezro of Carmel; Paarai the Arbite;
text.Rotherham: Hezro, the Carmelite, Paarai, the Arbite;
text.KJV: 23:35 Hezrai the Carmelite, Paarai the Arbite,
text.NABRE: Hezrai, from Carmel; Paarai the Arbite;

text.NRSV: Igal son of Nathan of Zobah; Bani the Gadite;
text.Rotherham: Igal, son of Nathan, of Zobah, Bani, the Gadite;
text.KJV: 23:36 Igal the sonne of Nathan of Zobah, Bani the Gadite,
text.NABRE: Igal, son of Nathan, from Zobah; Bani the Gadite;

text.NRSV: Zelek the Ammonite; Naharai of Beeroth, the armor-bearer of Joab son of Zeruiah;
text.Rotherham: Zelek, the Ammonite,—Naharai, the Beerothite, armour bearers to Joab son of Zeruiah;
text.KJV: 23:37 Zelek the Ammonite, Naharai the Berothite, armour-bearer to Ioab the sonne of Zeruiah,
text.NABRE: Zelek the Ammonite; Naharai, from Beeroth, the armor-bearer of Joab, son of Zeruiah;

text.NRSV: Ira the Ithrite; Gareb the Ithrite;
text.Rotherham: Ira, the Ithrite, Gareb, the Ithrite;
text.KJV: 23:38 Ira an Ithrite, Gareb an Ithrite,
text.NABRE: Ira, from Jattir; Gareb, from Jattir;

text.NRSV: Uriah the Hittite—thirty-seven in all.
text.Rotherham: Uriah, the Hittite, In all, thirty and seven.
text.KJV: 23:39 Uriah the Hittite: thirtie and seuen in all.
text.NABRE: Uriah the Hittite—thirty-seven in all.

text.NRSV: Again the anger of the Lord was kindled against Israel, and he incited David against them, saying, “Go, count the people of Israel and Judah.”
text.Rotherham: And again was the anger of Yahweh kindled against Israel,—so that he suffered David to be moved against them, saying, Go, count Israel and Judah.
text.KJV: 24:1 And againe the anger of the Lord was kindled against Israel, and hee mooued Dauid against them, to say, Goe, number Israel and Iudah.
text.NABRE: Chapter 24 David’s Census; the Plague. The Lord ’s anger against Israel flared again, and he incited David against them: “Go, take a census of Israel and Judah.”

text.NRSV: So the king said to Joab and the commanders of the army, who were with him, “Go through all the tribes of Israel, from Dan to Beer-sheba, and take a census of the people, so that I may know how many there are.”
text.Rotherham: The king, therefore, said unto Joab, captain of the force, who was with him—Go to and fro, I pray thee, throughout all the tribes of Israel, from Dan even unto Beer -sheba, and number ye the people,—so shall I know the sum of the people.
text.KJV: 24:2 For the king said to Ioab the captaine of the hoste, which was with him, Goe now through all the tribes of Israel, from Dan euen to Beer-sheba, and number ye the people, that I may know the number of the people.
text.NABRE: The king therefore said to Joab and the leaders of the army who were with him, “Tour all the tribes of Israel from Dan to Beer-sheba and register the people, that I may know their number.”

text.NRSV: But Joab said to the king, “May the Lord your God increase the number of the people a hundredfold, while the eyes of my lord the king can still see it! But why does my lord the king want to do this?”
text.Rotherham: Then said Joab unto the king—Yea, Yahweh thy God add unto the people, how many soever they be, a hundredfold, and [suffer] the eyes of my lord the king, to see it,—but, my lord the king, wherefore doth he find pleasure in this thing?
text.KJV: 24:3 And Ioab sayde vnto the King, Now the Lord thy God adde vnto the people (how many soeuer they be) an hundred folde, and that the eyes of my lorde the king may see it: but why doeth my lord the king delight in this thing?
text.NABRE: But Joab replied to the king: “May the Lord your God increase the number of people a hundredfold for my lord the king to see it with his own eyes. But why does it please my lord to do a thing of this kind?”

text.NRSV: But the king’s word prevailed against Joab and the commanders of the army. So Joab and the commanders of the army went out from the presence of the king to take a census of the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Notwithstanding, the word of the king prevailed against Joab, and over the captains of the force,—so Joab went forth, with the captains of the force, before the king, to number the people—Israel.
text.KJV: 24:4 Notwithstanding, the kings word preuailed against Ioab, and against the captaines of the hoste: and Ioab and the captaines of the host went out from the presence of the king, to number the people of Israel.
text.NABRE: However, the king’s command prevailed over Joab and the leaders of the army, so they left the king’s presence in order to register the people of Israel.

text.NRSV: They crossed the Jordan, and began from Aroer and from the city that is in the middle of the valley, toward Gad and on to Jazer.
text.Rotherham: And they passed over the Jordan,—and encamped in Aroer, on the right side of the city, that is in the midst of the ravine of Gad, even towards Jazer.
text.KJV: 24:5 ¶ And they passed ouer Iordane, and pitched in Aroer, on the right side of the citie that lieth in the midst of the riuer of Gad, and toward Iazer.
text.NABRE: Crossing the Jordan, they began near Aroer, south of the city in the wadi, and turned in the direction of Gad toward Jazer.

text.NRSV: Then they came to Gilead, and to Kadesh in the land of the Hittites; and they came to Dan, and from Dan they went around to Sidon,
text.Rotherham: Thus came they to Gilead, and unto the land of Tahtim-hodshi,—and came to Dan-jaan, and round about Zidon;
text.KJV: 24:6 Then they came to Gilead, and to the land of Tahtim-Hodshi; and they came to Dan-Iaan, and about to Zidon,
text.NABRE: They continued on to Gilead and to the district below Mount Hermon. Then they proceeded to Dan; from there they turned toward Sidon,

text.NRSV: and came to the fortress of Tyre and to all the cities of the Hivites and Canaanites; and they went out to the Negeb of Judah at Beer-sheba.
text.Rotherham: and entered the fortress of Tyre, and all the cities of the Hivites, and of the Canaanites,—and they went out to the South of Judah, even to Beer-sheba.
text.KJV: 24:7 And came to the strong holde of Tyre, and to all the cities of the Hiuites, and of the Canaanites: and they went out to the South of Iudah, euen to Beer-sheba.
text.NABRE: going to the fortress of Tyre and to all the cities of the Hivites and Canaanites, and ending up in the Negeb of Judah, at Beer-sheba.

text.NRSV: So when they had gone through all the land, they came back to Jerusalem at the end of nine months and twenty days.
text.Rotherham: So, when they had gone to and fro throughout all the land, they came, at the end of nine months and twenty days, unto Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 24:8 So when they had gone through all the land, they came to Ierusalem at the ende of nine moneths, and twentie dayes.
text.NABRE: Thus they toured the whole land, reaching Jerusalem again after nine months and twenty days.

text.NRSV: Joab reported to the king the number of those who had been recorded: in Israel eight hundred thousand soldiers able to draw the sword, and those of Judah were five hundred thousand.
text.Rotherham: And Joab delivered up the sum of the number of the people, unto the king,—and there were found to be, in Israel, eight hundred thousand men of valour, drawing the sword,—and, the men of Judah, five hundred thousand men.
text.KJV: 24:9 And Ioab gaue vp the summe of the number of the people vnto the king, and there were in Israel eight hundred thousand valiant men that drewe the sword: and the men of Iudah were fiue hundred thousand men.
text.NABRE: Joab then reported the census figures to the king: of men capable of wielding a sword, there were in Israel eight hundred thousand, and in Judah five hundred thousand.

text.NRSV: But afterward, David was stricken to the heart because he had numbered the people. David said to the Lord, “I have sinned greatly in what I have done. But now, O Lord, I pray you, take away the guilt of your servant; for I have done very foolishly.”
text.Rotherham: And the heart of David smote him, after he had reckoned up the people,—and David said unto Yahweh—I have sinned greatly, in what I have done, Now, therefore, O Yahweh, take away, I beseech thee, the iniquity of thy servant, for I have done very foolishly.
text.KJV: 24:10 ¶ And Dauids heart smote him, after that hee had numbred the people: and Dauid sayde vnto the Lord, I haue sinned greatly in that I haue done: and nowe I beseech thee, O Lord, take away the iniquitie of thy seruant, for I haue done very foolishly.
text.NABRE: Afterward, however, David regretted having numbered the people. David said to the Lord: “I have sinned grievously in what I have done. Take away, Lord, your servant’s guilt, for I have acted very foolishly.”

text.NRSV: When David rose in the morning, the word of the Lord came to the prophet Gad, David’s seer, saying,
text.Rotherham: And, when David arose in the morning, the word of Yahweh, had come unto Gad the prophet, the seer of David, saying:
text.KJV: 24:11 For when Dauid was vp in the morning, the word of the Lord came vnto the Prophet Gad Dauids Seer, saying,
text.NABRE: When David rose in the morning, the word of the Lord came to the prophet Gad, David’s seer, saying:

text.NRSV: “Go and say to David: Thus says the Lord: Three things I offer you; choose one of them for me to do to you.”
text.Rotherham: Go and speak unto David—Thus, saith Yahweh, Three things, do I offer thee,—choose thee one of them, that I may do it unto thee.
text.KJV: 24:12 Goe and say vnto Dauid, Thus saith the Lord, I offer thee three things; chuse thee one of them, that I may doe it vnto thee.
text.NABRE: Go, tell David: Thus says the Lord: I am offering you three options; choose one of them, and I will give you that.

text.NRSV: So Gad came to David and told him; he asked him, “Shall three years of famine come to you on your land? Or will you flee three months before your foes while they pursue you? Or shall there be three days’ pestilence in your land? Now consider, and decide what answer I shall return to the one who sent me.”
text.Rotherham: So Gad came in unto David, and told him,—and said to him: Shall there come unto thee seven years of famine in thy land? Or, for three months, wilt thou flee before thine enemies while, they, pursue thee? Or shall there be, for three days, pestilence in thy land? Now, consider and see, what, answer, I shall return to him that sent me.
text.KJV: 24:13 So Gad came to Dauid, and told him, and said vnto him, Shall seuen yeeres of famine come vnto thee in thy land? or wilt thou flee three moneths before thine enemies, while they pursue thee? or that there be three dayes pestilence in thy land? Now aduise, and see what answere I shall returne to him that sent me.
text.NABRE: Gad then went to David to inform him. He asked: “Should three years of famine come upon your land; or three months of fleeing from your enemy while he pursues you; or is it to be three days of plague in your land? Now consider well: what answer am I to give to him who sent me?”

text.NRSV: Then David said to Gad, “I am in great distress; let us fall into the hand of the Lord, for his mercy is great; but let me not fall into human hands.”
text.Rotherham: And David said unto Gad—I am in a great strait,—let us fall, I pray thee, into the hand of Yahweh, for, manifold, are, his compassions, but, into the hand of man, let me not fall.
text.KJV: 24:14 And Dauid saide vnto Gad, I am in a great strait: let vs fall now into the hand of the Lord (for his mercies are great,) and let me not fall into the hand of man.
text.NABRE: David answered Gad: “I am greatly distressed. But let us fall into the hand of God, whose mercy is great, rather than into human hands.”

text.NRSV: So the Lord sent a pestilence on Israel from that morning until the appointed time; and seventy thousand of the people died, from Dan to Beer-sheba.
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh sent forth a pestilence throughout Israel, from the morning even unto the time appointed,—and there died of the people, from Dan even unto Beer-sheba, seventy thousand men.
text.KJV: 24:15 ¶ So the Lord sent a pestilence vpon Israel, from the morning, euen to the time appointed: and there died of the people from Dan euen to Beersheba, seuentie thousand men.
text.NABRE: Thus David chose the plague. At the time of the wheat harvest it broke out among the people. The Lord sent plague over Israel from morning until the time appointed, and from Dan to Beer-sheba seventy thousand of the people died.

text.NRSV: But when the angel stretched out his hand toward Jerusalem to destroy it, the Lord relented concerning the evil, and said to the angel who was bringing destruction among the people, “It is enough; now stay your hand.” The angel of the Lord was then by the threshing floor of Araunah the Jebusite.
text.Rotherham: But, when the messenger stretched out his hand towards Jerusalem, to destroy it, then relented Yahweh as to the evil, and he said to the messenger who was destroying the people—Enough! now, stay thy hand. And, the messenger of Yahweh, was by the threshing-floor of Araunah the Jebusite.
text.KJV: 24:16 And when the Angel stretched out his hand vpon Ierusalem to destroy it, the Lord repented him of the euill, and said to the Angel that destroyed the people, It is ynough: stay now thine hand. And the Angel of the Lord was by the threshing place of Araunah the Iebusite.
text.NABRE: But when the angel stretched forth his hand toward Jerusalem to destroy it, the Lord changed his mind about the calamity, and said to the angel causing the destruction among the people: Enough now! Stay your hand. The angel of the Lord was then standing at the threshing floor of Araunah the Jebusite.

text.NRSV: When David saw the angel who was destroying the people, he said to the Lord, “I alone have sinned, and I alone have done wickedly; but these sheep, what have they done? Let your hand, I pray, be against me and against my father’s house.”
text.Rotherham: Then said David unto Yahweh, when he saw the messenger who was smiting the people, yea he said—Lo! I, have sinned, and, I, have done perversely, but what have, these sheep, done? Let thy hand, I pray thee, be against me, and against the house of my father!
text.KJV: 24:17 And Dauid spake vnto the Lord when he saw the Angel that smote the people, and said, Loe, I haue sinned, and I haue done wickedly: but these sheepe, what haue they done? Let thine hand, I pray thee, be against mee, and against my fathers house.
text.NABRE: When David saw the angel who was striking the people, he said to the Lord: “It is I who have sinned; it is I, the shepherd, who have done wrong. But these sheep, what have they done? Strike me and my father’s family!”

text.NRSV: Gad came to David that day and said to him, “Go up and erect an altar to the Lord on the threshing floor of Araunah the Jebusite.”
text.Rotherham: And Gad came unto David, on that day,—and said unto him—Go up, rear thou unto Yahweh an altar, in the threshing-floor of Araunah the Jebusite.
text.KJV: 24:18 ¶ And Gad came that day to Dauid, and said vnto him, Goe vp, reare an Altar vnto the Lord, in the threshing floore of Araunah the Iebusite.
text.NABRE: David Offers Sacrifices. On the same day Gad went to David and said to him, “Go and set up an altar to the Lord on the threshing floor of Araunah the Jebusite.”

text.NRSV: Following Gad’s instructions, David went up, as the Lord had commanded.
text.Rotherham: So David went up, according to the word of Gad, as Yahweh had commanded.
text.KJV: 24:19 And Dauid, according to the saying of Gad, went vp, as the Lord commanded.
text.NABRE: According to Gad’s word, David went up as the Lord had commanded.

text.NRSV: When Araunah looked down, he saw the king and his servants coming toward him; and Araunah went out and prostrated himself before the king with his face to the ground.
text.Rotherham: And Araunah looked out, and saw the king, and his servants, passing over unto him—so Araunah went forth, and did homage unto the king, with his face to the ground.
text.KJV: 24:20 And Araunah looked, and saw the King and his seruants comming on toward him: and Araunah went out, and bowed himselfe before the King on his face vpon the ground.
text.NABRE: Now Araunah looked down and saw the king and his servants coming toward him while he was threshing wheat. So he went out and bowed down before the king, his face to the ground.

text.NRSV: Araunah said, “Why has my lord the king come to his servant?” David said, “To buy the threshing floor from you, in order to build an altar to the Lord, so that the plague may be averted from the people.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Araunah, Wherefore, hath my lord the king come unto his servant? And David said—To buy of thee the threshing-floor, to build an altar unto Yahweh, that the plague may be stayed from off the people.
text.KJV: 24:21 And Araunah said, Wherefore is my lord the King come to his seruant? and Dauid saide, To buy the threshing floore of thee, to build an Altar vnto the Lord, that the plague may be stayed from the people.
text.NABRE: Then Araunah asked, “Why does my lord the king come to his servant?” David replied, “To buy the threshing floor from you, to build an altar to the Lord, that the plague may be withdrawn from the people.”

text.NRSV: Then Araunah said to David, “Let my lord the king take and offer up what seems good to him; here are the oxen for the burnt offering, and the threshing sledges and the yokes of the oxen for the wood.
text.Rotherham: Then said Araunah unto David, Let my lord the king accept it and cause to ascend what is good in his own eyes,—see! the oxen for the ascending-sacrifice, and the threshing-sledges and ox-yokes for wood.
text.KJV: 24:22 And Araunah said vnto Dauid, Let my lord the King take and offer vp what seemeth good vnto him: Beholde, here be oxen for burnt sacrifice, and threshing instruments, and other instruments of the oxen for wood.
text.NABRE: But Araunah said to David: “Let my lord the king take it and offer up what is good in his sight. See, here are the oxen for burnt offerings, and the threshing sledges and the yokes of oxen for wood.

text.NRSV: All this, O king, Araunah gives to the king.” And Araunah said to the king, “May the Lord your God respond favorably to you.”
text.Rotherham: The whole, did Araunah give, as a king to a king. And Araunah said unto the king, Yahweh thy God, accept thee!
text.KJV: 24:23 All these things did Araunah, as a king, giue vnto the King: and Araunah saide vnto the King, The Lord thy God accept thee.
text.NABRE: All this does Araunah give to the king.” Araunah then said to the king, “May the Lord your God accept your offering.”

text.NRSV: But the king said to Araunah, “No, but I will buy them from you for a price; I will not offer burnt offerings to the Lord my God that cost me nothing.” So David bought the threshing floor and the oxen for fifty shekels of silver.
text.Rotherham: But the king said unto Araunah—Nay! but I will, buy, it of thee, for a price, and will not cause to ascend unto Yahweh my God, offerings that have cost me nothing. So David bought the threshing-floor, and the oxen, for fifty shekels of silver;
text.KJV: 24:24 And the King said vnto Araunah, Nay, but I will surely buy it of thee at a price: neither will I offer burnt offerings vnto the Lord my God, of that which doeth cost mee nothing. So Dauid bought the threshing floore, and the oxen, for fiftie shekels of siluer.
text.NABRE: The king, however, replied to Araunah, “No, I will buy it from you at the proper price, for I cannot sacrifice to the Lord my God burnt offerings that cost me nothing.” So David bought the threshing floor and the oxen for fifty silver shekels.

text.NRSV: David built there an altar to the Lord, and offered burnt offerings and offerings of well-being. So the Lord answered his supplication for the land, and the plague was averted from Israel.
text.Rotherham: and David built there an altar unto Yahweh, and caused to go up ascending-sacrifices and peace-offerings,—then was Yahweh entreated for the land, and the plague was stayed from Israel.
text.KJV: 24:25 Aud Dauid built there an Altar vnto the Lord, and offered burnt offerings, and peace offerings: so the Lord was intreated for the land, and the plague was stayed from Israel.
text.NABRE: Then David built an altar to the Lord there, and sacrificed burnt offerings and communion offerings. The Lord granted relief to the land, and the plague was withdrawn from Israel.

18.33: Chapter 19 The king was shaken, and went up to the room over the city gate and wept. He said as he wept, “My son Absalom! My son, my son Absalom! If only I had died instead of you, Absalom, my son, my son!”
